hook.text: Stand in a windowless elevator—you can't tell if it's resting on Earth or accelerating in space.

buildup.text: Einstein realized that gravitational acceleration and rocket acceleration feel identical.

discovery.text: This means gravity and acceleration are fundamentally the same thing. Physics can't distinguish them.

twist.text: This seemingly simple insight took Einstein eight years of hard math to develop into a full theory.

climax.text: The equivalence principle is the foundation of general relativity—Einstein's masterwork.

punchline.text: Gravity or acceleration? Physics says they're identical.

quiz.question: What does the equivalence principle state?
options: Gravity and acceleration are physically indistinguishable
All masses fall at the same rate
